mission.title: dify: MCP Server supports header configuration
mission.description: MCP Server supports header configuration. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current dify deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dify version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
helm list -n dify 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dify installation.

steps.title: Review dify configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dify configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get configmap -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dify
```
### Self Checks

### 1. Is this request related to a challenge you're experiencing? Tell me about your story.

steps.title: Apply the fix for MCP Server supports header configuration
steps.description:
## Summary

This PR adds support for custom HTTP headers in MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool providers, allowing users to configure additional authentication or configuration headers when connecting to MCP servers. This enhancement improves the flexibility of MCP tool integration by supporting various authentication methods and server-specific requirements.

### Key Changes

-
Added `headers` parameter support in MCP tool provider creation and update endpoints
-
Added `headers` column to

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get events -n dify --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "MCP Server supports header configuration" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

This PR adds support for custom HTTP headers in MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool providers, allowing users to configure additional authentication or configuration headers when connecting to MCP servers.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dify installed or the issue environment reproducible.
